topics.title: Playwright end-to-end test harness added to snake game
topics.trigger: The project's technical task list had a single outstanding item: set up a Playwright test harness. The developer wanted a structured plan reviewed before any code was written.
topics.response:
- `playwright.config.ts` was created to configure Chromium as the sole browser target, auto-start the Vite dev server on a pinned port, and enable trace capture and screenshots on failure. Three npm scripts (`test`, `test:ui`, `test:report`) were added to `package.json`. The `.gitignore` was updated to exclude test artifacts.
- Four spec files were written under `tests/e2e/`: `menu.spec.ts` covers initial menu rendering and leaderboard navigation; `gameplay.spec.ts` covers new-game readouts, pause toggle, and reset; `game-over.spec.ts` drives the snake into a wall to trigger a deterministic death and verifies the death-banner freeze and game-over screen flow; `iron-snake.spec.ts` verifies the HUD swap when Iron Snake mode is enabled. A shared `helpers.ts` provides reusable navigation flows and screen selectors.
- A GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/playwright.yml`) was added to run the full suite on every push and pull request to `main`, uploading the HTML report as a build artifact retained for seven days.
topics.decisions:
- **Chromium only to start**: The harness targets a single browser rather than the full Playwright cross-browser matrix. Adding Firefox and WebKit later is straightforward, but starting lean keeps CI fast and avoids flakiness from browser-specific quirks before the suite is proven stable.
- **Keyboard-and-DOM testing strategy**: The game renders entirely on a canvas with no state exposed to the page, so tests cannot inspect game internals directly. The approach drives the game through keyboard input and asserts on observable DOM signals — screen visibility toggled by a CSS class, and text readouts for score, level, and goal — rather than canvas pixels. This keeps tests resilient to visual changes while still covering real user flows.
- **Deterministic death over score-based assertions**: The game-over test steers the snake into the right wall from its fixed starting position rather than waiting for a score increment or fruit collection. Wall collision is guaranteed within a bounded number of game ticks, making the test reliable without any random-seed control or internal game hooks. A `window.__game` handle for deeper determinism was explicitly deferred as a follow-up.
topics.todo: - A `window.__game` dev-mode handle was discussed as a future addition to enable seeded fruit spawns and forced game states for deeper deterministic testing. Deferred by explicit agreement.

recap:
The snake game now has a full automated test suite that runs on every code change. Eight end-to-end tests cover the main menu, single-player game startup, pause and reset behavior, death and game-over flow, and Iron Snake mode — all driven through keyboard input and verified against visible on-screen text and screen transitions. A mid-run fix was needed for the Iron Snake goal readout, which displays as a formatted string rather than a bare number.

The suite runs automatically in GitHub Actions on every push and pull request to the main branch at no cost, with the full test report saved as a downloadable artifact for seven days. Developers can also run the suite locally with a single command, or open an interactive visual runner to step through tests in a real browser.

e2eTestGuide.title: Playwright test suite runs and passes
e2eTestGuide.preconditions: Node 18 or later installed; dependencies installed by running "npm ci" in the project folder; Playwright browsers installed by running "npx playwright install chromium".
steps: Open a terminal in the project folder.
Wait for the suite to finish (it may take up to a minute on first run while the dev server starts).
Check the terminal output when the run completes.
Optionally type "npm run test:report" and press Enter to open the HTML report in your browser.
expectedResults: The terminal should show all tests passing with no failures (look for a green summary line such as "28 passed").
The HTML report should open and list every test as passed, with no red entries.
No browser window should remain open after the run finishes.
